name: AI Faces
description: Face-oriented AI capability contributions for image and video analysis.
author: Cove Team

tutorialTopics.title: AI Faces
tutorialTopics.description: Face detection, embeddings, clustering, and reference-pack matching.

slides.imageAlt: The Faces page after running face analysis
slides.title: Recognize people in your library
slides.caption: Run AI with face analysis on your videos and images. AI Faces detects faces, then groups the same person's appearances together into face identities you can review.
slides.bodyMarkdown: You don't tag people one clip at a time. AI Faces finds every face, clusters the ones that look like the same person, and collects them on the **Faces** page for you to review and link to performers.

slides.imageAlt: The Faces page with recognized people
slides.title: Review faces on the Faces page
slides.caption: Open the Faces page from the top navigation. Each tile is one recognized person, showing a sample image, how many times they were detected, and the top suggested performer with a confidence score.
slides.bodyMarkdown: The page defaults to the **Unlinked** filter — the faces still waiting for you to assign them. Work through these to connect the people Cove found to performers in your library.

slides.imageAlt: A face card with Link and Compare actions
slides.title: Link a face to a performer
slides.caption: When a face has a good suggestion, link it directly. To double-check first, use Compare to see the face and the suggested performer side by side before you confirm.
slides.bodyMarkdown:
Your options on a face:

- **Link** — accept the top suggestion immediately.
- **Compare** — review evidence side by side, then **Confirm link**, **Reject**, or **Skip**.
- **Create new performer** — when the person isn't in your library yet, make a performer from the face (optionally using the face image).

If two reference sources disagree about who a face is, you'll be prompted to pick the right performer or **merge** the matches.
points: [blue] Compare — verify side by side before confirming
Create a new performer when the person isn't in your library yet

slides.imageAlt: The Faces page where you can delete unwanted faces
slides.title: Delete faces you don't want
slides.caption: Not every detected face is worth keeping. Delete low-quality faces and people you don't care about so the Faces page stays focused on identities that matter.
slides.bodyMarkdown:
Good candidates to delete:

- **Blurry or partial detections** that don't clearly show a real person.
- **Background or one-off people** you'll never want to track.

Delete a single face from its detail page's menu, or select several on the Faces page and delete them together. Deleting removes the face and its AI data but does **not** affect performers or your media. It can't be undone.

slides.title: Recognize known performers with SAIE packs
slides.caption: A SAIE reference pack is a prebuilt set of known performer faces. Import one and AI Faces can suggest those performers automatically, instead of you identifying everyone by hand.
slides.bodyMarkdown: Go to **Settings → Extensions → AI → AI Faces** and use **Upload .saie pack** in the Reference Face DB section. After import, Cove reconciles your existing faces against the pack and starts suggesting matches. You can download community SAIE packs from the docs site.
slides.imageAlt: AI Faces settings Reference Face DB section with the Upload .saie pack button
AI Faces settings pairing a SAIE pack with a metadata server
slides.title: Pair each pack with a metadata server
slides.caption: For each SAIE pack you import, it's recommended to configure the matching metadata server. Then accepting a face match can also pull that performer's image, bio, and aliases.
slides.bodyMarkdown: Configure servers under **Settings → Data Sources**. In AI Faces settings, the **Update existing performers from metadata servers** option means that when you accept a reference match, Cove also refreshes that performer from the originating server — so a single confirmation both links the face *and* fills in rich performer details.
